nodes.x: I will extol thee, my God, O king; and I will bless thy name for ever and ever.
Every day will I bless thee; and I will praise thy name for ever and ever.
I will praise thee, O Lord my God, with all my heart: and I will glorify thy name for evermore.
I will make thy name to be remembered in all generations: therefore shall the people praise thee for ever and ever.
Thus will I bless thee while I live: I will lift up my hands in thy name.
I will praise thee, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will shew forth all thy marvellous works.
Every day will I bless thee; and I will praise thy name for ever and ever.
I will extol thee, my God, O king; and I will bless thy name for ever and ever.
I will bless the Lord at all times: his praise shall continually be in my mouth.
Great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised; and his greatness is unsearchable.
Great is our Lord, and of great power: his understanding is infinite.
For great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised: he also is to be feared above all gods.
Great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised in the city of our God, in the mountain of his holiness.
Great is his wisdom, marvelous are his ways, and the extent of his doings none can find out.
Praise him for his mighty acts: praise him according to his excellent greatness.
One generation shall praise thy works to another, and shall declare thy mighty acts.
This shall be written for the generation to come: and the people which shall be created shall praise the Lord.

Verily, verily, I say unto you, even as you desire of me, so shall it be done unto you ; and, if you desire you shall be the means of doing much good in this generation. Say nothing but repentance unto this generation. Keep my commandments, and assist to bring forth my work according to my commandments, and you shall be blessed.

Verily, verily, I say unto you, even ns you desire of me, so shall it be unto you; and if you desire, you shall be the means of doing much good in this generation. Say nothing but repeotance unto this generation: keep my commandments and assist to bring forth rny work according
I will speak of the glorious honour of thy majesty, and of thy wondrous works.

With sentiments of high consideration and esteem for your
Majesty and the people of your realm, I have the honor to subscribe
myself,
Bless the Lord, O my soul. O Lord my God, thou art very great; thou art clothed with honour and majesty.
And men shall speak of the might of thy terrible acts: and I will declare thy greatness.
Say unto God, How terrible art thou in thy works! through the greatness of thy power shall thine enemies submit themselves unto thee.
And now, I beseech thee, let the power of my Lord be great, according as thou hast spoken, saying,
And I will yet be more vile than thus, and will be base in mine own sight: and of the maidservants which thou hast spoken of, of them shall I be had in honour.
But I say unto you, That every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment.
Therefore, verily I say unto you, lift up your voices unto this people; speak the thoughts that I shall put into your hearts, and you shall not be confounded before men;
They shall abundantly utter the memory of thy great goodness, and shall sing of thy righteousness.
They shall speak of the glory of thy kingdom, and talk of thy power;
And also the Lord will remember the prayers of the righteous, which have been put up unto him for them.
Yea, they shall sing in the ways of the Lord: for great is the glory of the Lord.
I will sing of the mercies of the Lord for ever: with my mouth will I make known thy faithfulness to all generations.
And there shall ye remember your ways, and all your doings, wherein ye have been defiled; and ye shall lothe yourselves in your own sight for all your evils that ye have committed.
The Lord is gracious, and full of compassion; slow to anger, and of great mercy.
The Lord is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and plenteous in mercy.
But thou, O Lord, art a God full of compassion, and gracious, longsuffering, and plenteous in mercy and truth.
He hath made his wonderful works to be remembered: the Lord is gracious and full of compassion.
Gracious is the Lord, and righteous; yea, our God is merciful.
For his merciful kindness is great toward us: and the truth of the Lord endureth for ever. Praise ye the Lord.
The Lord is good to all: and his tender mercies are over all his works.
Hear me, O Lord; for thy lovingkindness is good: turn unto me according to the multitude of thy tender mercies.
Great are thy tender mercies, O Lord: quicken me according to thy judgments.
The Lord is good, a strong hold in the day of trouble; and he knoweth them that trust in him.
For the Lord is good; his mercy is everlasting; and his truth endureth to all generations.
All thy works shall praise thee, O Lord; and thy saints shall bless thee.
And the heavens shall praise thy wonders, O Lord: thy faithfulness also in the congregation of the saints.
Let the people praise thee, O God; let all the people praise thee.
Praise ye the Lord. Praise, O ye servants of the Lord, praise the name of the Lord.
I will praise thee for ever, because thou hast done it: and I will wait on thy name; for it is good before thy saints.
They shall speak of the glory of thy kingdom, and talk of thy power;
All the kings of the earth shall praise thee, O Lord, when they hear the words of thy mouth.
And now, Lord, behold their threatenings: and grant unto thy servants, that with all boldness they may speak thy word,
Then shall the King say unto them on his right hand, Come, ye blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world:
They shall abundantly utter the memory of thy great goodness, and shall sing of thy righteousness.
To make known to the sons of men his mighty acts, and the glorious majesty of his kingdom.

Who being the brightness of his glory, and the express image of his person, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;
The Lord of hosts hath purposed it, to stain the pride of all glory, and to bring into contempt all the honourable of the earth.
And in his estate shall stand up a vile person, to whom they shall not give the honour of the kingdom: but he shall come in peaceably, and obtain the kingdom by flatteries.
Thy kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and thy dominion endureth throughout all generations.

And Thy power and kingship and greatness abide for ever and ever,
    And throughout all generations Thy dominion:
    And all the heavens are Thy throne for ever,
    And the whole earth Thy footstool for ever and ever.
Thou, O Lord, remainest for ever; thy throne from generation to generation.
And thine house and thy kingdom shall be established for ever before thee: thy throne shall be established for ever.
Thy throne is established of old: thou art from everlasting.
But thou, O Lord, shalt endure for ever; and thy remembrance unto all generations.
The Lord upholdeth all that fall, and raiseth up all those that be bowed down.
The Lord openeth the eyes of the blind: the Lord raiseth them that are bowed down: the Lord loveth the righteous:
And the loftiness of man shall be bowed down, and the haughtiness of men shall be made low; and the Lord alone shall be exalted in that day.
And he who is faithful shall overcome all things, and shall be lifted up at the last day.

May the Lord preserve his people in righteousness and in holiness of heart, that they may cause to be felled to the earth all who shall seek to slay them because of power and secret combinations, even as this man hath been felled to the earth.
And saith unto him, All these things will I give thee, if thou wilt fall down and worship me.
The eyes of all wait upon thee; and thou givest them their meat in due season.
These wait all upon thee; that thou mayest give them their meat in due season.

And I will leave thee thrown into the wilderness, thee and all the fish of thy rivers: thou shalt fall upon the open fields; thou shalt not be brought together, nor gathered: I have given thee for meat to the beasts of the field and to the fowls of the heaven.
And the Lord said, Who then is that faithful and wise steward, whom his lord shall make ruler over his household, to give them their portion of meat in due season?
Now therefore up by night, thou and the people that is with thee, and lie in wait in the field:
Thou openest thine hand, and satisfiest the desire of every living thing.
In whose hand is the soul of every living thing, and the breath of all mankind.
That thou givest them they gather: thou openest thine hand, they are filled with good.
Thou shalt call, and I will answer thee: thou wilt have a desire to the work of thine hands.
If thou be righteous, what givest thou him? or what receiveth he of thine hand?
And the Spirit said unto me: Behold, what desirest thou?
The Lord is righteous in all his ways, and holy in all his works.
Thy righteousness also, O God, is very high, who hast done great things: O God, who is like unto thee!
He loveth righteousness and judgment: the earth is full of the goodness of the Lord.
The heavens declare his righteousness, and all the people see his glory.
His work is honourable and glorious: and his righteousness endureth for ever.
The Lord is nigh unto all them that call upon him, to all that call upon him in truth.
But the word is very nigh unto thee, in thy mouth, and in thy heart, that thou mayest do it.
But know that the Lord hath set apart him that is godly for himself: the Lord will hear when I call unto him.
The Lord is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.
And he goeth up into a mountain, and calleth unto him whom he would: and they came unto him.
And he said unto them, Go ye into all the world, and preach the gospel to every creature.
He will fulfil the desire of them that fear him: he also will hear their cry, and will save them.
Then shall they cry unto the Lord, but he will not hear them: he will even hide his face from them at that time, as they have behaved themselves ill in their doings.
His heart is established, he shall not be afraid, until he see his desire upon his enemies.
Behold, the eye of the Lord is upon them that fear him, upon them that hope in his mercy;
He will bless them that fear the Lord, both small and great.
The secret of the Lord is with them that fear him; and he will shew them his covenant.
The Lord preserveth all them that love him: but all the wicked will he destroy.
The Lord says the wicked shall slay the wicked, and he orders it so. I pray God that he will preserve the righteous, and endue his people who
And he leadeth away the righteous into precious lands, and the wicked he destroyeth, and curseth the land unto them for their sakes.

For the time speedily cometh that the Lord God shall cause a great division among the people, and the wicked will he destroy; and he will spare his people, yea, even if it so be that he must destroy the wicked by fire.
This is one thing, therefore I said it, He destroyeth the perfect and the wicked.
My mouth shall speak the praise of the Lord: and let all flesh bless his holy name for ever and ever.
Blessed be the name of our God; let us sing to his praise, yea, let us give thanks to his holy name, for he doth work righteousness forever.

thy name shall have the honor, the praise and glory, for we ask all in Jesus' name. And unto thee and our blessed Lord and Savior and to the Holy Spirit be all power, might and dominion, worlds without end. Amen.
I will greatly praise the Lord with my mouth; yea, I will praise him among the multitude.
Save us, O Lord our God, and gather us from among the heathen, to give thanks unto thy holy name, and to triumph in thy praise.
